concepts.name: Bridged room mirroring
concepts.description: Every bridged remote chat requires a Matrix room to mirror it, and the central design question is who owns that room.

concepts.name: Portal room
concepts.description: A Matrix room created, owned, and state-enforced by a bridge to mirror a remote chat.

concepts.name: Plumbing room
concepts.description: A user-created Matrix room into which bridges are invited as guests to attach remote chats.

concepts.name: E2EE–bridging tension
concepts.description: Bridges must read message plaintext to carry it, so end-to-end encrypting a bridged room hands the bridge ciphertext and silences it.

concepts.name: Bridge bot power level 50
concepts.description: Granting bridge bots power level 50 clears state_default so they can attach plumbing, without the admin-level power that would let them demote the room owner.

concepts.name: Standalone Discord plumbing bridge
concepts.description: The standalone Discord bridge predates the bridgev2 rewrite and still supports plumbing, attaching a Discord channel to a user-owned room via a bridge command.

concepts.name: Relay webhooks
concepts.description: Relay webhooks let non-logged-in Matrix users appear in Discord under their own names and avatars instead of being quoted aloud by the bridge bot.

concepts.name: bridgev2 megabridge core
concepts.description: bridgev2 is the shared Go core the mautrix bridges were rewritten onto, centralizing portal, login, and relay behavior.

concepts.name: Split portals and the receiver
concepts.description: In bridgev2 every portal belongs to the specific login it was bridged through, called the receiver.

concepts.name: Receiver relay lock
concepts.description: Only the receiver login of a split portal may relay messages on behalf of users who are not logged in to the bridge.

concepts.name: Bot-first bridging order
concepts.description: Bridging through a dedicated bot login before any personal login makes the bot the receiver, so relay rights permanently follow the bot.

concepts.name: unset-relay nil-pointer panic
concepts.description: mautrix v0.27.0's unset-relay command crashed with a nil-pointer panic yet still cleared the relay setting as a side effect before dying.

concepts.name: Portal-only core design
concepts.description: The bridgev2 core supports only bridge-owned portal rooms; plumbing was deliberately designed out rather than left unimplemented.

concepts.name: Enforced portal sync
concepts.description: Exclusive bridge ownership lets the bridge keep a room's name, avatar, and membership authoritatively in sync, reverting outside edits including direct database changes.

concepts.name: Relay appservice
concepts.description: A custom Matrix appservice that listens in bridge-owned portal rooms and re-speaks every message into a user-owned hub room, rebuilding plumbing one layer above the bridges.

concepts.name: Identity puppets
concepts.description: Namespaced appservice users that re-speak relayed messages while wearing the original sender's display name and avatar.

concepts.name: Hub/spoke configuration separation
concepts.description: Relay configuration names portal rooms as spokes and one hub room separately, and the hub must never appear in the portal list or the relay feeds on its own output.

concepts.name: Relay echo loop
concepts.description: A relay that hears its own output relays its relays, spawning exponentially multiplying copies of every message.

concepts.name: Self-puppet ward
concepts.description: The relay must ignore messages from its own puppet namespace because those messages are its own output.

concepts.name: Bridge-ghost ward
concepts.description: The relay must ignore bridge bots and their ghost users, since the megabridge already delivered those messages and re-relaying them builds a cross-bridge loop.

concepts.name: Attribution-prefix ward
concepts.description: A 'Name: ' attribution prefix is a wire-format flag meaning some relay already spoke the message once, so prefixed messages must never be relayed again.

concepts.name: Per-room event identity
concepts.description: Matrix event identity is per-room: a relayed message becomes a distinct new event in every room it lands in, with no global notion of 'this message'.

concepts.name: Relay event map
concepts.description: A SQLite map of sibling event IDs across rooms lets the relay re-point replies and reactions room by room so threading survives relaying.

concepts.name: Double puppeting
concepts.description: When a bridge-logged-in user sends from their native app the message arrives as their real Matrix account, and the relay must look up their platform puppet to present the right identity in each world.

concepts.name: Hybrid bridging architecture
concepts.description: The complete design uses plumbing where bridges permit it, puppet relaying where they force portals, and echo wards throughout to bind many platforms into one room.

relationships.label: same exclusive-ownership principle at another scale
both restore sender identity across a bridge
